name: New Orleans Butter Pralines
recipeInstructions:
1. Place 2 large parchment-paper sheets on baking sheets; butter the parchment paper.
2. Set aside.
3. In a heavy, 2-quart saucepan over medium heat, combine all ingredients except vanilla.
4. Bring the mixture to a boil, stirring often.
5. Cook to the soft-ball stage (234°F / 114°C). Remove from heat and stir in vanilla.
6. Stir briskly until the mixture loses its glossy sheen.
7. Working quickly, drop the mixture by tablespoons onto the buttered paper.
8. Let pralines cool completely before removing them from the paper.
9. Store at room temperature in an airtight container.
Makes 24 pralines
New Orleans Pralines--and that's pronounced PRAH-leens--are unique among pralines. They contain butter and milk to give them a softer, almost chewy texture.
